task: Add CSS custom property override to lock progress bar and navigation buttons to neon-green color

changes.description: Added CSS custom property overrides to lock gradient colors to neon-green

verification.cssFileUpdated: PASSED - CSS file contains the color lock rule
verification.buildTest: PASSED - bun run build completed successfully with only performance warnings (expected)
verification.scopedCorrectly: CONFIRMED - Rule is scoped to .dynamic-content-carousel wrapper, will not affect other carousel blocks

implementationDetails.approach: CSS custom property override scoped to .dynamic-content-carousel
affectedElements: Navigation button hover ::before background (uses same gradient tokens)
Autoplay toggle button hover (uses same .carousel-nav-btn class)
reasoning: Uses CSS specificity: block-scoped custom properties override page-level accent color variants
Maintains DRY principle by referencing --color-neon-green and --color-blue theme tokens
Does not modify shared swiper.css (would break color theming for other carousel blocks)
If neon-green palette is ever updated in colors.css, this block automatically picks up the change

colorValues.note: These are the default neon-green gradient pair from the theme colors

summary: Successfully added CSS custom property override to lock DynamicContentCarousel progress bar and navigation buttons to neon-green color. The rule is scoped to .dynamic-content-carousel wrapper so it doesn't affect other carousel blocks that share the same swiper.css styles. Build completed successfully. The block will now always display neon-green gradient regardless of the page's accent color setting.
nextSteps: QA team should test on a page using a non-neon-green accent color (blue, purple, orange)
Verify progress bar shows neon-green-to-blue gradient (not the page accent color)
Verify navigation button hover shows neon-green-to-blue gradient
Verify other carousel blocks on the same page still respect the page accent color
Verify editor preview matches frontend rendering
